title: podman machine init apparently ignores ignition file completely
body:
### Issue Description

The `--ignition-path` option to `podman machine init` seems to have no effect at all at least in Windows WSL2 where I'm testing now. The option is accepted by the command but it seems to completely ignore the provided file.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I created an ignition file using butane and am trying to initialize a Windows WSL2 podman machine based on it, using the following command:

    podman machine init --ignition-path=D:\Podman\podman.ign

The command actually initializes the machine, but the ignition file will be completely ignored. This is the output I get

```
PS D:\Podman> podman machine init --ignition-path=D:\Podman\podman.ign
Extracting compressed file
Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
Configuring system...
Generating public/private ed25519 key pair.
Your identification has been saved in podman-machine-default
Your public key has been saved in podman-machine-default.pub
The key fingerprint is:
SHA256:AZYb40CzZUeAFbk/I66wn61C7pzELW9HkJ3lpIYwf/I root@GGDE-014018
The key's randomart image is:
+--[ED25519 256]--+
|   .ooO*o        |
|  o o*=oo        |
|   +.* @.        |
|    * X ..       |
|     * .S        |
| ...  E +        |
| o= .o . o       |
| oo*.oo          |
| .=+*+.          |
+----[SHA256]-----+
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start
```

As can be seen, contrary to what the documentation says, the command creates a SSH key while, on the other hand, the effects of the ignition file (adding  certificate authorities) are not to be seen in the machine. Furthermore, I can specify any file path, existing or not, to the `--ignition-path` option and the command runs just the same. Nothing happens either if I specify a path to a file that is clearly not an ignition file.

Does this feature work at all in Windows?

### Describe the results you received

See above.

### Describe the results you expected

The ignition file being used to create the machine as described by the documentation.

### Upstream Latest Release

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
that is correct because the backing OS is not Fedora CoreOS (uses ignition).  It is a custom Fedora OS.  It is not a bug that it ignores it frankly.  The bug is that the option should not be available for the WSL implementation AND the documentation should also state that.

I'm also unaware about other options like --image-path.
@n1hility perhaps we could chat about this one?
@baude thanks for the information. The time I lost down this blind alley (fortunately not much) won't come back, but it would be good to have this at least documented property.
@baude I'm thinking logging a warning might be the way to go here, alternatively we could hard error?

Thank you @MartinSoto for pointing this out, I was just considering to give this a try.

So, alternatively: what would be the "correct" way to set custom certifcate authorities, http/https proxy... at "podman machine init" time, if not via an ignition file?

In the case of proxy settings. These are automatically picked up from system / user env vars as part of podman machine start. For custom CA certs you can either manually install them via podman machine ssh or wsl prompt. Or, alternatively you can create your own custom image and pass it to image path. If you do the latter you can derive from the code for the official podman image here: https://github.com/containers/podman-wsl-fedora
